description:
You are assisting a researcher who is setting up a small, isolated Python
    environment to work on several text-based datasets.  
    The researcher would like you to do the following, **using only normal
    user privileges** (no sudo):

    1. Create a Python virtual-environment directory at  
       /home/user/venvs/research_env

    2. Activate that environment and install exactly version 2.31.0 of the
       PyPI package “requests”.  
       • No other packages need to be installed.  
       • The version **must** be pinned precisely to 2.31.0.

    3. Produce a single-file manifest,  
       /home/user/research/installed_packages.csv, with **this exact
       2-line CSV format** (including the comma between the fields and a final
       newline at the end of the second line):

           package,version
           requests,2.31.0

       Notes on the format that will be automatically checked:
       • Line-1 header must read exactly “package,version”.  
       • Line-2 must read exactly “requests,2.31.0”.  
       • There must be **only** these two lines; no extra blank lines or
         whitespace.  
       • The file must end with a single Unix newline (LF) character after
         line 2.

    4. Make sure both the virtual-environment directory and the CSV manifest
       exist at the exact paths given above when you finish.

    The system will run automated tests that:

    • Check that /home/user/venvs/research_env exists and contains a valid
      virtual-environment structure (e.g. the “bin/python” executable).  
    • Verify that “requests” version 2.31.0 is installed *inside* that
      virtual-environment (the tests will execute
      “/home/user/venvs/research_env/bin/pip show requests”).  
    • Confirm that /home/user/research/installed_packages.csv exists and that
      its contents match the 2-line format shown above **exactly**.

    No root access is available, so rely solely on
    “python3 -m venv” and “pip install --no-input …”.
    A typical solution takes about 3-4 shell commands.
truth:
/home/user/venvs/research_env                (directory must exist)
    /home/user/venvs/research_env/bin/python     (file must exist)
    /home/user/venvs/research_env/bin/pip        (file must exist; when run,
                                                  “pip show requests” must
                                                  return Version: 2.31.0)

    /home/user/research/installed_packages.csv   (file must exist with exactly
                                                  the following 34 bytes,
                                                  including newline characters):

        7061636b6167652c76657273696f6e0a
        72657175657374732c322e33312e300a

    (The hex above decodes to:
        package,version\n
        requests,2.31.0\n)

    The automated grader will:
    1. Verify the virtual-env path exists.
    2. Execute
         /home/user/venvs/research_env/bin/pip show requests
       and parse for “Version: 2.31.0”.
    3. Read the CSV file and compare it byte-for-byte to the 34 bytes listed
       above.
name: task_000000_2b7a4e31
